result.summary: Apigee Registry exposes one legacy Google Cloud REST API at `https://apigeeregistry.googleapis.com/`, authenticated with Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 access tokens, plus access through the `gcloud` CLI.
result.description: Apigee Registry is a Google Cloud service for storing and managing machine-readable API descriptions, versions, specs, deployments, and related metadata. The documentation notes this legacy API is no longer supported and recommends Apigee API hub APIs for current functionality.

gcp_oauth.label: Google Cloud access token
gcp_oauth.setup: Use a Google Cloud account with the Apigee Registry API enabled for your project. The easiest path is to install the [Google Cloud CLI](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install), then run [`gcloud auth login`](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/login) for a user token or [`gcloud auth application-default login`](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/application-default/login) for application default credentials. For service-to-service use, create a service account in the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/), grant Apigee Registry permissions, and obtain an OAuth 2.0 access token via Google Cloud Application Default Credentials.

variables.name: project
variables.description: Google Cloud project ID in resource names like `projects/{project}/locations/{location}`.

variables.name: location
variables.description: Google Cloud location in resource names like `projects/{project}/locations/{location}`.
